word_list.word: art is long and life is short
word_list.pronunciation: ˈɑːɹt ɪz lˈɑːŋ ænd lˈaɪf ɪz ʃˈɔːɹt

senses.definition: used to imply that artistic creations have the ability to endure long after their creators have passed away, making them a kind of immortality

examples.x: The poet wrote, knowing that art is long and life is short, and hoping his verses would help him leave some small mark on the world beyond the span of his own life.

word_list.word: ashes to ashes (, dust to dust|)

senses.definition: used to imply that all living things will eventually die and return to the earth as dust, emphasizing the idea of mortality and the fleeting nature of human life
used to imply that once someone or something is dead, it no longer poses a threat or danger, aiming to reassure someone  who is afraid of a situation that their fear may be unfounded
used to emphasize that all humans are equal in the face of death, and that the pursuit of material wealth or status is ultimately meaningless in the face of mortality

examples.x: The fact that 'death is the great leveler' is a reminder to live our lives with purpose and to focus on the things that truly matter, such as our relationships with others and our own personal growth.

senses.definition: used to suggest that when a person dies, any debts or obligations they may have had during their lifetime are considered to be settled or paid in full

word_list.word: dying is (just|) as natural as living
word_list.pronunciation: dˈaɪɪŋ ɪz dʒˈʌst æz nˈætʃɚɹəl æz lˈɪvɪŋ

senses.definition: used to emphasize the idea that death is a natural part of the human experience, and that it should be accepted as such

examples.x: My grandmother used to say that dying is as natural as living as a way of comforting me when my pet goldfish passed away.

senses.definition: used emphasize the importance of treating all individuals with respect and dignity, as they will ultimately be equal in death regardless of any differences that may have existed during their lifetimes

examples.x: My father believed that the end makes all equal and raised me to treat strangers and neighbors with the same kindness and respect, without judgment.

senses.definition: used to imply that when a person is good or virtuous, it is always too soon for them to die, regardless of their age

examples.x: "The good die young," said the pastor sadly at Robert's funeral, for even at the age of 80, his kindness, faith and generosity made his passing feel far too soon.

word_list.word: in the midst of life we are in death
word_list.pronunciation: ɪnðə mˈɪdst ʌv lˈaɪf wiː ɑːɹ ɪn dˈɛθ

senses.definition: used to emphasize the fragility of human life and the idea that death is an inescapable part of the human experience

examples.x: The car accident that nearly took his life made him realize that in the midst of life we are in death, and that we should not take our existence for granted.

senses.definition: used to express the idea that death is the ultimate end, and that nothing can compare to it or reverse its effects

examples.x: When his beloved wife passed away, the widower knew stone-dead hath no fellow and that nothing could replace her or bring her back.

word_list.word: there is a remedy for everything except death
word_list.pronunciation: ðɛɹ ɪz ɐ ɹˈɛmədi fɔːɹ ˈɛvɹɪθˌɪŋ ɛksˈɛpt dˈɛθ

senses.definition: used to emphasize that  while there may be solutions or cures for many of the difficulties we face in life, death is the one force that cannot be avoided or overcome

examples.x: Trauma surgeons work tirelessly to save lives, but ultimately they realize there is a remedy for everything except death and must accept that some injuries are simply too severe.

word_list.word: life is not separate from death, it only looks that way
word_list.pronunciation: lˈaɪf ɪz nˌɑːt sˈɛpɹət fɹʌm dˈɛθ ɪt ˈoʊnli lˈʊks ðæt wˈeɪ

senses.definition: used as a reminder that people's lives are part of a larger cycle of existence, and that death is a natural and inevitable part of that cycle

word_list.word: let the dead bury the dead
word_list.pronunciation: lˈɛt ðə dˈɛd bˈɛɹi ðə dˈɛd

senses.definition: used to emphasize the importance of living in the present moment and not becoming overly attached to the past or to the dead

examples.x: The wise woman advised me to let the dead bury the dead, counseling me to leave behind grief over past losses so I could open my heart to new experiences.

word_list.word: young men may die, but old men must die
word_list.pronunciation: jˈʌŋ mˈɛn mˈeɪ dˈaɪ bˌʌt ˈoʊld mˈɛn mˈʌst dˈaɪ

senses.definition: used to suggest that death is a natural and inevitable part of life, and that all people, regardless of age, will eventually die
used to suggest that it is pointless to accumulate material possessions in life because they cannot be taken with us after we die

examples.x: My grandfather often warned that shrouds have no pockets, encouraging me to spend wisely and value experiences over possessions.

senses.definition: used to express the idea that life is unfair, and that sometimes individuals who are the most deserving of a long and fulfilling life are taken too soon

examples.x: The tragic death of our good friend Tom at age 29 reminded us yet again that the good die young, and how unfair life can be to take someone so young with so much promise.
